_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: While everyone else's house was burning, she was quietly building the vault.

museum_objects.title: First 1850 Swiss Franc Coin
museum_objects.description: One of the first coins struck under Switzerland's newly unified federal currency system.
museum_objects.significance: While the rest of Europe was still arguing over borders and kings, Switzerland quietly agreed on one thing: a single, boring, reliable coin. That's Clara's whole personality in one object.
museum_objects.plain_language: One of the very first Swiss franc coins ever made, from the year Switzerland unified its money.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

display_alternatives.title: Medal Issued by the Swiss Cantons on the Birth of Princess Claude of France (1547–1575)
display_alternatives.match_text: Medal Issued by the Swiss Cantons on the Birth of Princess Claude of France (1547–1575) Medal 1547 Medals and Plaquettes

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so. | IMAGE QA FAIL: The image shows a watch, not a coin, so it does not depict a Swiss Franc coin.

museum_objects.title: Helvetia-Design Swiss Coinage
museum_objects.description: Swiss franc coins featuring the personification 'Helvetia,' designed by Friedrich Fisch.
museum_objects.significance: Helvetia is basically Clara's original face — calm, neutral, and completely uninterested in flattering anyone.
museum_objects.plain_language: Swiss coins featuring 'Helvetia,' the calm female figure who has represented Switzerland's currency for over 150 years.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Vreneli 20-Franc Gold Coin
museum_objects.description: An iconic Swiss gold coin nicknamed 'Vreneli,' widely held as a store of value and gift coin.
museum_objects.significance: When people around the world lost faith in paper money, they turned to this small gold coin — Clara's reputation for reliability made physical.
museum_objects.plain_language: A famous small gold coin that Swiss people have given as gifts and used as savings for over a century.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Latin Monetary Union Founding Documents
museum_objects.description: Treaty documents establishing the Latin Monetary Union, which standardized currencies among several European nations based on the franc.
museum_objects.significance: Other countries wanted to borrow Clara's stability so badly they built an entire treaty around copying her.
museum_objects.plain_language: An old treaty where several European countries agreed to base their money on the Swiss franc system.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Swiss National Bank Founding Charter
museum_objects.description: Founding charter establishing the Swiss National Bank as the country's sole currency-issuing authority.
museum_objects.significance: This is the paperwork that finally gave Clara one single, unified voice instead of dozens of competing regional banks.
museum_objects.plain_language: The founding document of the Swiss National Bank, which took over managing Switzerland's money in 1907.

world_map.map_description: Clara's franc was born from Swiss federal unification in 1850 and became the world's premier safe-haven currency, anchored in Bern, Zurich, and Basel.

nodes.label: Bern (Bundeshaus)
nodes.description: Switzerland's capital, where the 1850 currency unification and the 1907 SNB founding charter were enacted.

nodes.label: Zurich (Bahnhofstrasse)
nodes.description: One of the world's most prominent banking and finance streets, symbolic of Swiss financial stability.

nodes.label: Basel (BIS Tower)
nodes.description: Home to the Bank for International Settlements, the 'central bank for central banks.'

nodes.label: La Chaux-de-Fonds
nodes.description: Center of Swiss watchmaking precision, a cultural echo of the exacting standards behind the franc's reliability.

nodes.label: Geneva
nodes.description: International financial hub and home to numerous global banks holding Swiss franc reserves.

historical_map_source.title: SNB Monetary and Currency Policy Chronicle
